model.label: MiMo-V2-Flash
model.description: MiMo • Flash-tier model configured through a custom endpoint for fast inference and lightweight agent tasks.

pick.rationale: St. John's holds a slight edge in overall efficiency metrics, ranking 18th in Torvik and 20th in KenPom compared to Kansas's 22nd and 21st, with a more balanced offensive attack and a defense that has proven elite all season. While Kansas boasts a top-10 adjusted defense, their offense is less potent (56th-57th in efficiency) and they have shown vulnerability in close games against top competition, whereas St. John's has won 27 games and the Big East title under Rick Pitino. The underdog's path for Kansas relies on their defense forcing turnovers and winning the rebounding battle, but St. John's superior form, experience in a tougher conference, and recent championship pedigree make them the more reliable pick to advance.

reasoningStep.title: Investigate matchup, health, and recent form
reasoningStep.summary: I checked both teams' advanced ratings, reviewed their first-round opponents and performance, and searched for injury news. Both teams are healthy and in strong form, with St. John's coming off a Big East title and Kansas handling a low-major in the first round.
reasoningStep.evidence: St. John's ranks 18th (Torvik) and 20th (KenPom) with a 27-6 record and elite defense (adjde 95.1). Kansas ranks 22nd (Torvik) and 21st (KenPom) with a 23-10 record and top-10 defense (adjde 94.4). No significant injury reports found for either team. St. John's beat Northern Iowa (71st KenPom) in Round 1; Kansas beat California Baptist (no ratings) easily.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
